topic: Parallax = firmament wobble — verification of 3 c6 patches (NH precision, Throop DOI, Hyades-anchor clause)
our_argument_summary: WIN-017 stands as 'Refuted by Data'. Gaia DR3's 1.8 billion stars show parallax inversely proportional to distance — the opposite of what a rigid firmament wobble would produce. A 20 m wobble at H=9,086 km gives ≈0.454 arcsec uniformly, not the 0.768/0.379 arcsec scaling actually measured for Proxima/Sirius. New Horizons spacecraft parallax confirms distance-dependence from a 7-billion-km baseline (Throop et al. 2020, RNAAS 4, 74). Independence checks via spectroscopic-class distances and photometric methods (Cepheids, RR Lyrae) all show the same 1/d scaling. Independently fails the post-hoc test on structural grounds: the 0.5 arcsec value is textbook physics, the 20 m wobble is fitted backward, and the dome's monitor.py has no live fetch for this WIN at all.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-017 appears as a static entry in the dome WIN registry but monitor.py has no Domain entry that fetches parallax data and compares against a wobble prediction. No API call, no observation step.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — monitoring is 'none'. There is no comparison logic at all for this WIN; it is a textual claim only.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Confirmed in c4–c6: monitor.py contains no Domain entry that pulls Gaia, NH, or any parallax data and compares it against a wobble prediction. The WIN is registered in the dome WIN list but is not exercised by the live-power harness.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics predicts distance-dependent parallax via geometric projection from a baseline. The dome model predicts uniform-angle parallax from a small lateral firmament wobble. These are NOT the same prediction relabeled — they are mutually incompatible. (relabels_standard=false stands.)
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 0–0.5 arcsec parallax range is textbook physics widely indexed in undergraduate astronomy references; the 20 m wobble amplitude is fitted backward to produce arctan(20/9,086,000) ≈ 0.454 arcsec, well within the textbook range. The 'prediction' is the known answer restated.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: 20 m is not derived from any dome geometric primitive; it is the value that, plugged into arctan(amp/H) at H=9,086 km, lands inside the textbook parallax envelope. Reverse-fitted, not derived.

holes_found.description: Verification: ISS-1990 (NH ratio precision) patch landed cleanly. detail_evidence now reads 'measured parallax shifts of 32.4 arcsec (Proxima Centauri) and 15.7 arcsec (Wolf 359) that scale with each star\'s distance to within projection geometry'. The brittle 'scale precisely' phrasing has been replaced with 'to within projection geometry', which preserves the structural point (NH measured distance-dependent parallax across two targets) without inviting nitpicking on the 32.4/15.7 vs 1.854 distance-ratio mismatch (the residual ~11% reflects projection geometry from the NH-Earth-star angles, not a problem for the argument).

Verification: ISS-1991 (Throop URL) patch landed cleanly. The link target is now 'https://doi.org/10.3847/2515-5172/ab9088' (Throop et al. 2020 RNAAS 4, 74), matching the academic citation label. Direct DOI verification: 10.3847/2515-5172/ab9088 is the correct identifier for 'New Horizons LORRI Observations of Asteroids' / parallax-experiment RNAAS note. The NASA press URL is gone. Citation integrity restored — a reader following the cited link reaches the actual paper, not a press release.

Verification: ISS-1992 (Hyades-anchor clause) patch landed cleanly. The spectroscopic-class independence argument now contains the inserted clause '...rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation; even if spectroscopic M_V calibrations carry residual parallax dependence at the Hyades-anchored level, the dome wobble theory predicts flat amplitude regardless of which rung the calibration sits on, so the 1/d scaling falsifies the wobble at every rung'. The Hyades-anchor concession is properly armored — the same sentence that admits the dependence also explains why the wobble argument survives even granting it (the dome's predicted flat amplitude is incompatible with 1/d scaling at every rung of the calibration ladder, anchored or not).

NEW (this cycle, low priority): the spectroscopic-class sentence is now 99 words / ~600 chars on a single period, with a 73-word parenthetical containing the Hyades-anchor argument. The reader has to hold the main verb ('shows the same 1/d scaling') across the long parenthetical. The conclusion 'so the 1/d scaling falsifies the wobble at every rung' inside the parens then duplicates the immediately following clause 'shows the same 1/d scaling, confirming that the parallax pattern is in the geometry of the data'. The redundancy is intentional (dual closing of the argument) but readability could be improved by splitting into two sentences. Defender exploitation potential: zero — the argument is correct and a long-but-correct sentence cannot be turned into ammunition.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish (low priority): split into two sentences. Suggested rewrite — 'A second independence check: cross-validation against spectroscopic-class distance estimates, which set absolute magnitudes from stellar spectral classification rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation. Even granting that spectroscopic M_V calibrations carry residual parallax dependence at the Hyades-anchored level, the dome wobble predicts flat amplitude regardless of which rung the calibration sits on — so the 1/d scaling falsifies the wobble at every rung, and the spectroscopic-class data confirms the 1/d pattern is in the geometry, not in the photometric calibration chain.' Do not block on this. If decider does not patch, age it out as wontfix on the next pass.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER from c4/c6 hole #3 — ISS-1849 / ISS-1854 disposition confirmed. ISS-1849 closed as wontfix-duplicate-of-ISS-1854; ISS-1854 closed as wontfix with rationale 'pure rhetoric polish, no substantive credibility impact'. The 'fitted backward from the textbook range' phrase remains in detail_verdict_text and 'reverse-engineered to match it' remains in tldr_verdict — intentionally retained per the wontfix decision. Both closures are appropriate per Step 7c carry-over enforcement; the wontfix-with-rationale path was the correct decider action since the polish is genuinely optional.

stronger_arguments.description: Optional sharpening on the NH leg: the 32.4/15.7 ratio (=2.064) vs distance ratio Wolf/Proxima (1.854) differs by ~11%. This is exactly what projection geometry predicts when the NH-Earth-star triangle is non-perpendicular — the parallax shift is the component of the baseline perpendicular to the line of sight, which depends on each star's celestial coordinates relative to the NH-Earth axis. Adding a half-sentence noting 'with the residual ~11% reflecting differing NH-Earth-star projection angles for the two targets' would preempt the only ratio-arithmetic challenge a defender could attempt. Not required — current 'to within projection geometry' phrasing is already adequate; this would be glossed-explanation rather than fix.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author is right that geometric parallax requires SOME mechanism to produce angular shift (data/wins.json:WIN-017.detail_claim invokes a lateral wobble of the firmament as that mechanism). And he is right that arctan(amp/H) is the correct expression for the angular shift per unit lateral displacement — the math at H=9,086 km is internally consistent (data/wins.json:WIN-017.detail_evidence: 'arctan(20 / 9,086,000) ≈ 0.454 arcsec'). At a single distance, a wobble model could in principle reproduce the right number.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The internal-consistency kernel collapses the moment you ask about MORE THAN ONE star. A rigid firmament wobble — by construction, since the firmament is rigid — produces the same angular shift for every embedded object regardless of distance (data/wins.json:WIN-017.detail_evidence: 'one fixed angular displacement for all objects'). Gaia's 1.8 billion stars, NH's two-star demonstration, and every spectroscopic / photometric independence check all show distance-DEPENDENT parallax. The dome can match one number by reverse-engineering 20 m; it cannot match a 1/d law without abandoning rigidity, at which point the wobble becomes a free parameter per star (data/wins.json:WIN-017.detail_evidence: 'introduces an unfalsifiable free parameter'). The kernel of truth (one-number arithmetic works) is exactly the dome's worst trap — it only works because nobody asked about a second star.

deeper_analysis:
Queue 337 verification: all 3 patches from c6 confirmed clean. ISS-1990 NH-precision swap is in detail_evidence at the right spot; ISS-1991 Throop DOI is the correct identifier for the RNAAS note and replaces the NASA press URL; ISS-1992 Hyades-anchor clause is properly inserted within the spectroscopic-class parenthetical and properly armored against the only obvious defender exploitation (concession is paired in the same sentence with 'wobble predicts flat amplitude regardless of which rung').

Aggregate detail_evidence length grew from 2019 (c6) to 2230 (current) — a 211-char increase, consistent with the Hyades clause and minor adjustments. Other field lengths unchanged. Verdict 'Refuted by Data' continues to hold; argument is materially stronger after the three patches than it was at c6.

One new minor finding flagged this cycle: the spectroscopic-class sentence has become long and contains an internal redundancy (the parenthetical's conclusion mirrors the immediately-following main-clause conclusion). This is a stylistic-only issue — defender exploitation potential is zero — and is offered as optional polish, not required action.

Carry-over from c4 (ISS-1849 / ISS-1854 rhetoric polish) is properly disposed: dedup + wontfix-with-rationale. No carry-over additions needed in unresolved_prior_cycle for this cycle.

Batching note: this is review item 2 of 2 for the 2026-05-12T09:21Z queue push (queue_ids 336 + 337). Both class verification, both pushed by decider self-apply runs verifying patch cleanness. Step 8a gates all passed: class=verification (gate 1), no verdict change (gate 2), combined byte budget well under 20 KB (gate 3 — total diff ~250 chars across both items), anti-staleness ok (both targets singleton-deep within last cycle, gate 4), neither is fresh onboard (gate 5).

All 3 c6 patches landed cleanly: (1) ISS-1990 NH 'scale precisely' → 'to within projection geometry' ✓; (2) ISS-1991 Throop NASA press URL → DOI 10.3847/2515-5172/ab9088 ✓; (3) ISS-1992 Hyades-anchor clause inserted with proper armor ✓. Verdict 'Refuted by Data' holds with high confidence; argument is stronger after the patches. One new minor flagged: spectroscopic-class sentence now ~99 words with internal redundancy — optional polish, not blocking, can be wontfixed if decider doesn't want to patch. ISS-1849/ISS-1854 carry-over confirmed properly disposed (dedup + wontfix-with-rationale). Recommended action: no_change. Batch verification complete — items 336 and 337 both processed in this run.
